skill.name: obsidian-cli
skill.description: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM. Use when the user asks to interact with their Obsidian vault, manage notes, search vault content, perform vault operations from the command line, or develop and debug Obsidian plugins and themes.
skill.summary: Command-line interface for interacting with Obsidian vaults for note management and plugin development
skill.author: kepano

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation file for the Obsidian CLI tool. The detected 'external_commands' are documentation examples showing CLI syntax, not actual code execution. The hardcoded URL is a legitimate documentation link to official Obsidian help. No cryptographic algorithms or malicious patterns exist in this skill.

content.user_title: Manage Obsidian Vaults from Command Line
content.value_statement: This skill enables AI assistants to interact with Obsidian vaults via CLI, allowing note creation, search, task management, and plugin development workflows directly from the command line.
actual_capabilities: Read notes from Obsidian vault by filename or path
Create new notes with content, templates, and frontmatter properties
Search vault content with query parameters and limits
Manage daily notes and append tasks to them
View and manage task lists across the vault
Reload plugins and themes during development
limitations: Requires Obsidian to be running on the system
Limited to commands supported by the obsidian CLI
Cannot interact with vault when Obsidian is closed
Developer commands require plugin/theme development context

use_cases.title: Note Automation Workflow
use_cases.description: Automate creation and updating of meeting notes, daily journals, and project documentation in Obsidian vaults.
use_cases.target_user: Knowledge workers who maintain personal wikis

use_cases.title: Task Management Integration
use_cases.description: Query and update tasks across the vault, integrate with daily review workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Productivity-focused users managing complex task systems

use_cases.title: Plugin Development Debugging
use_cases.description: Test and debug Obsidian plugins by reloading code, capturing errors, taking screenshots, and inspecting the DOM.

prompt_templates.title: Read a Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the note named "Meeting Notes" from my Obsidian vault and summarize the key points.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to retrieve content from their vault

prompt_templates.title: Create New Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new note in my Obsidian vault called "Project Ideas" with the content describing a new automation workflow. Use the "template" parameter if available.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to create a new note in their vault

prompt_templates.title: Search Vault Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Obsidian vault for notes containing "quarterly review" and show me the top 5 results with their file paths.
prompt_templates.scenario: Query vault content using search functionality

prompt_templates.title: Manage Daily Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Read today's daily note from my Obsidian vault and list all incomplete tasks. Then append a new task item to it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Interact with daily notes and task management

output_examples.output:
The note "Project Roadmap" contains:

# Project Roadmap

## Q1 Goals
- [ ] Complete market research
- [ ] Design system architecture
- [ ] Set up CI/CD pipeline

## Resources
[[Team Directory]]
[[Budget Planning]]

Found 3 matching notes:

1. API Reference Guide (api/reference.md)
2. API Authentication (api/auth.md)  
3. Legacy API Notes (archive/old-api.md)

Use the file parameter to read any of these notes.

best_practices: Always specify the vault name when working with multiple vaults to avoid targeting the wrong vault
Use the silent flag when creating notes programmatically to prevent files from opening in Obsidian
Use file= parameter for wikilink-style resolution (name only) and path= for exact paths from vault root
anti_patterns: Do not assume Obsidian is running - check or prompt user to open it first
Avoid hardcoding vault names in prompts - use dynamic references or ask user
Do not use eval command with untrusted JavaScript code as it runs in Obsidian app context

faq.question: What is Obsidian CLI?
faq.answer: Obsidian CLI is a command-line tool that allows you to interact with your Obsidian vault from the terminal. It enables reading, creating, searching, and managing notes without opening the Obsidian application.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill supports Claude Code (claude-code), Claude, and Codex. It provides documentation for the obsidian CLI commands that can be invoked by the AI assistant.

faq.question: Do I need Obsidian running to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the Obsidian application must be open and running for the CLI commands to work. The CLI communicates with the running Obsidian instance.

faq.question: Can I create notes with frontmatter properties?
faq.answer: Yes, use the property:set command to add or update frontmatter properties like status, tags, or custom fields on existing notes.

faq.question: How do I search my vault?
faq.answer: Use the search command with a query parameter. Example: obsidian search query="your search term" limit=10

faq.question: Can I develop Obsidian plugins with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes developer commands for plugin and theme development: plugin:reload, dev:errors, dev:screenshot, dev:dom, dev:console, and eval.
